summary: Security hardening pass (iteration_8): 42 backend tests pass (100%) — regression suite (places/search curated 23 food / 8 drinks with sponsored-first ordering, cuisine+price filters, GET /restaurants=31, /cuisines, /spin, create/delete, google_url present on all) + new security suite (test_security.py): zip_code '12'/'abcde' -> 422, valid + omitted zip ok, category='banana' coerced to food (23 items), /api/places/photo returns 404 without GOOGLE_API_KEY and rejects non-'places/' names (path traversal safe), no 'key=' string in /api/places/search response body, images are unsplash URLs on curated path, RestaurantCreate rejects empty name / name>120 / empty cuisine with 422, rate limiter returns 429 after >20 rapid /api/restaurants POSTs from same IP. Frontend smoke: Home loads, Food/Drinks toggle works, spin in drinks mode returned sponsored 'Pearl & Pour' drink card with Reviews & ratings + Spin again + Clear buttons, 8 nearby drink spots, Add spot in header, AdUnit renders 0 ins/adsbygoogle elements (PUB_ID empty).
critical_code_review_comments: In-memory rate limiter (_RL_BUCKETS defaultdict) is per-process — won't survive backend restart and won't share across replicas if scaled. Fine for MVP/single-node but note for prod.
Rate limiter keys off request.client.host which behind reverse proxy will be the ingress IP; consider trusting X-Forwarded-For (with allowlist) so per-user limits work behind Kubernetes ingress.

The 20/min create limit is shared between POST /api/restaurants and DELETE /api/restaurants/{id} via the same _RL_BUCKETS dict — bursts of writes can lock out reads' companion deletes; may want separate buckets per route.

test_security.py::TestRateLimit::test_create_rate_limit_429 intentionally exhausts the 20/min create budget for its IP — when the full suite runs, any create-based tests that follow in the same 60s window may see 429s. This surfaced 2 collateral 429s in test_sponsored during the full run; re-running test_sponsored.py in isolation passed 4/4. Consider isolating RateLimit test to a dedicated suite or clearing the bucket in a pytest fixture teardown.
PlacesSearchRequest silently coerces unknown category to 'food' (per spec) — good, but consider logging the coercion so bad clients get feedback.
test_restaurants.py still has hardcoded fallback URL for REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (line 5) — minor deviation from 'no defaults' rule (carried over from prior iterations).
seed_data_creation: Rate-limit test creates ~20 TEST_RL_* rows; cleaned up post-run. Curated seed remains 23 food + 8 drinks = 31.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Rate limits: create/delete=20/min per IP, places/search=60/min per IP. When running the full pytest suite the RateLimit test will consume the create budget for ~60s; run test_sponsored/test_restaurants/test_drinks BEFORE RateLimit, or sleep 60s between, or deselect TestRateLimit and run it standalone. /api/places/photo requires GOOGLE_API_KEY AND name starting with 'places/' — else 404. Google API key intentionally unset so places/search always takes curated path.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app.
